growth_records.medium: Defined minimal medium with deoxyribose (10 mM) as sole carbon source
growth_records.cite: Price et al. Oxidative Pathways of Deoxyribose and Deoxyribonate Catabolism. mSystems (2019). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00297-18. [PMC6365646]
growth_records.snippet: P. simiae grows much more slowly on deoxyribose than on deoxyribonate, with a doubling time of around 12 h instead of around 2 h (Fig. 2)
growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h = ln2 / doubling_time_h = 0.693/12 = 0.0578 h-1. Doubling time explicitly reported as approximate ('around 12 h') from growth curves in Fig. 2; carbon source concentration (10 mM) taken from the Fig. 2 legend. All bacterial growth in this study performed at 30C (Materials and Methods); oxygenation/pH not explicitly stated for this assay.

growth_records.medium: Defined minimal medium with deoxyribonate (10 mM) as sole carbon source
growth_records.cite: Price et al. Oxidative Pathways of Deoxyribose and Deoxyribonate Catabolism. mSystems (2019). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00297-18. [PMC6365646]
growth_records.snippet: P. simiae grows much more slowly on deoxyribose than on deoxyribonate, with a doubling time of around 12 h instead of around 2 h (Fig. 2)
growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h = ln2 / doubling_time_h = 0.693/2 = 0.3466 h-1. Doubling time explicitly reported as approximate ('around 2 h') from growth curves in Fig. 2; carbon source concentration (10 mM) taken from Fig. 2 legend. All bacterial growth in this study performed at 30C (Materials and Methods); oxygenation/pH not explicitly stated for this assay.
